title: Verus-Ethereum Bridge signature or quorum validation failure historical bridge incident

historical_exploit.attack_summary: Blockaid detected an ongoing exploit on the Verus-Ethereum Bridge. The attacker drained approximately $11.58 million in assets (including ~1,625 ETH, ~103.6 tBTC, and ~147k USDC). The funds were swapped and consolidated into a drainer wallet (e.g., 0x65Cb8b128Bf6e690761044CCECA422bb239C25F9). This is a cross-chain bridge incident affecting the bridge infrastructure, not the core Verus blockchain. The project had recently issued an urgent update, but the exploit still occurred. Funds remain in the attacker's control as of the latest reports. On May 22, PeckShield's monitoring revealed that the exploiter of the Verus cross-chain bridge has returned 4,052.4 ETH (valued at around $8.5 million) to the team's designated address. This recovery accounts for 75% of the total plundered funds, while the remaining 25% (approximately 1,350 ETH) is being retained in the hacker's wallet as a bug bounty.
historical_exploit.attacker_profile: External attacker or unprivileged caller as described by the SlowMist incident row; exact actor permissions must be re-verified before reporting.
preconditions: A bridge, cross-chain router, gateway, wrapped-asset minter, liquidity pool, or validator/proof path accepts attacker-controlled message, proof, signature, transaction, deposit, configuration, or accounting input.
The accepted input releases existing bridge-held value, mints unbacked wrapped assets, miscredits a deposit, or lets a caller move assets beyond entitlement.
The target mapping must be reachable without relying on privileged key compromise, DNS/frontend phishing, social-account compromise, or pure rug-pull behavior.

value_path.asset_or_reward: bridge-held assets, wrapped assets, bridge liquidity, withdrawals, refunds, or backed cross-chain liabilities
value_path.how_value_was_extracted: Blockaid detected an ongoing exploit on the Verus-Ethereum Bridge. The attacker drained approximately $11.58 million in assets (including ~1,625 ETH, ~103.6 tBTC, and ~147k USDC). The funds were swapped and consolidated into a drainer wallet (e.g., 0x65Cb8b128Bf6e690761044CCECA422bb239C25F9). This is a cross-chain bridge incident affecting the bridge infrastructure, not the core Verus blockchain. The project had recently issued an urgent update, but the exploit still occurred. Funds remain in the attacker's control as of the latest reports. On May 22, PeckShield's monitoring revealed that the exploiter of the Verus cross-chain bridge has returned 4,052.4 ETH (valued at around $8.5 million) to the team's designated address. This recovery accounts for 75% of the total plundered funds, while the remaining 25% (approximately 1,350 ETH) is being retained in the hacker's wallet as a bug bounty.
value_path.why_it_paid: The historical row describes real loss, attempted bridge theft, unbacked minting, unauthorized withdrawal, or bridge-accounting failure that can be useful only if BridgeIndex has a matching current live-value extraction path.

mapping.why_this_might_map: BridgeIndex exposes bridge deposits, bot-gas accounting, withdrawal creation/approval/execution, refunds, token/resource mappings, relayer voting, and proposal execution. This historical primitive is useful only if it maps to extracting current BNB/USDT ledger value or backed bridge liabilities from the deployed BridgeIndex system.

notes: SlowMist attack method: Smart Contract Vulnerability.
Selected for scanned because: bridge infrastructure exploit with asset drain; mechanics need recovery
This seed is not proof that BridgeIndex is vulnerable; Workflow 7 must reject it unless the primitive maps to non-admin extraction of a current live ledger row.
